name: Goldstar Pawn & Jewelry
description_short: Miami pawn shop buying, selling, and pawning jewelry, gold, silver, coins, and used electronics since 1998. Located in the Miami-Dade area.
description_long:
Goldstar Jewelry & Pawn is a Florida-incorporated business that has been operating in Miami since April 1998, giving it roughly 27 years in the local pawn and secondhand valuables market. Based at 548 NW 57th Ave in the Miami-Dade area, the shop serves a broad cross-section of the Greater Miami community. It is registered as an active Florida corporation and is listed in business directories including Manta and Yelp. No BBB profile was found for this specific Miami location, and the business does not appear to hold specialty industry certifications.

The shop operates as a full-service pawn and jewelry business. Core offerings include collateral-based pawn loans on valuables, outright buying and selling of gold and silver, jewelry repair, coin dealing, and transactions in used electronics. Customers can bring in items to receive a cash offer — either as an outright sale or as collateral for a short-term pawn loan — or browse the shop's existing inventory of pre-owned goods. Like most pawn shops, loan terms and purchase offers are item-dependent and negotiated on the spot; no published pricing schedule is available.

What distinguishes Goldstar Jewelry & Pawn is its Google rating: 5.0 out of 5 stars based on 317 reviews, which is exceptionally high for a pawn shop and suggests consistent customer satisfaction over a large number of interactions. The shop's longevity — nearly three decades in one of the most competitive urban markets in Florida — also signals operational stability and community trust. Its service mix, which spans jewelry repair alongside buying and pawning, positions it as more of a full-service jewelry and loan shop than a bare-bones pawn outlet.

The main limitations are those inherent to the pawn model: loan amounts and buyout prices are entirely discretionary and offer-based, with no transparency on rates or terms until you walk in. There is no known website, no online portal, and no verified money-back guarantee. Consumers who need predictable loan amounts or want to comparison-shop rates remotely will find this difficult. That said, for Miami residents seeking fast cash on jewelry, gold, or coins — or looking for fairly priced pre-owned goods — Goldstar's strong review base and nearly three decades of operation make it a credible local option.
pros: Exceptional 5.0/5 Google rating from 317 reviews — rare for a pawn shop
Incorporated in Florida since April 1998, with ~27 years of continuous operation in Miami
Broad service mix: jewelry repair, gold/silver buying, coin dealing, and electronics in one location
Physical storefront in Miami-Dade at 548 NW 57th Ave with a listed phone number for direct contact
Active Florida corporation status confirms legitimate registered business standing
Serves both cash-need customers (pawn loans) and bargain shoppers (pre-owned inventory)
cons: No website found — customers cannot browse inventory, check hours, or verify services online
No published loan rates or pricing — all offers are in-person and discretionary
No BBB profile found for this Miami location, limiting independent third-party accountability data
No verified certifications, gemological credentials, or industry memberships found
Pawn loan model typically carries high effective interest rates compared to traditional lenders
best_for: Miami-Dade residents needing fast cash against jewelry, gold, or coins
Buyers looking for pre-owned jewelry or electronics at pawn shop prices
Customers with gold, silver, or coin collections to sell outright
Anyone needing in-person jewelry repair alongside buy/sell services
diagnosis: Goldstar Jewelry & Pawn in Miami is best suited for local residents who need quick, collateral-based cash loans or want to buy and sell gold, silver, jewelry, and coins in person — particularly those who prioritize a shop with a long track record and strong customer reviews. The main caveat is the complete absence of an online presence, published rates, or third-party accreditation, meaning all terms must be negotiated face-to-face with no ability to compare or verify pricing in advance.
services: Pawn loans on jewelry and valuables
On-site item appraisal for pawn or purchase offers
Trade-in of valuables for store credit or cash
